call_outcome: Scheduled a follow-up consultation for next Monday
customer_sentiment: Initially frustrated but became receptive and positive

strengths.title: Empathy and acknowledgment of customer's frustration
strengths.evidence: "I completely understand, and I'm sorry about that. I'll keep this brief. And if you prefer not to receive any more calls, I can make a note of that as well."

strengths.title: Effective needs discovery
strengths.evidence: "May I ask what didn't work for you in with those apps?" and customer response about lack of tracking

strengths.title: Clear explanation of product differentiation
strengths.evidence: "We provide personalized coaching instead of generic workout plans. Your coach checks in with you every week and adjust your workout and nutrition based on your progress."

strengths.title: Professional closing with scheduling next steps
strengths.evidence: "I'll send you the details by email today. And if Monday still works for you, we will schedule the consultation then."

improvements.title: Budget qualification was missing
improvements.suggestion: Include questions to understand the customer's budget or willingness to invest in the program to better tailor the offer.

improvements.title: More rapport building could enhance engagement
improvements.suggestion: Add more personalized or friendly comments to build stronger rapport beyond acknowledging frustration.

summary: The advisor demonstrated strong professionalism, empathy, and product knowledge throughout the call. She effectively acknowledged the customer's frustration, discovered needs by asking about previous experiences, and clearly explained the unique benefits of the product. The call was closed well with a scheduled follow-up. However, the advisor did not explore the customer's budget or financial readiness, which is an area for improvement. Overall, the call was handled well with no compliance or professionalism issues.
